title: taraxacum mucronatum
intro: Taraxacum mucronatum, a lesser-known species of dandelion, is a flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, roots, and flowers, which contain taraxasterol, a triterpene that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Taraxacum mucronatum include its ability to support liver function, improve digestion, and reduce blood pressure, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as jaundice, hepatitis, and hypertension. Taraxacum mucronatum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used as a food ingredient, adding a bitter flavor to salads and soups.
